vulnerabilities.description: Plain text storage of session memory data without encryption or access control
vulnerabilities.details: Memory file stored at .claude-flow/data/memory.json with no encryption. Could store sensitive trading context or API state.

vulnerabilities.description: Session context stored as plain JSON on disk without encryption
vulnerabilities.details: Session file at .claude-flow/sessions/current.json written with atomic operations (good) but no encryption.

vulnerabilities.description: Temporary files created in system temp directory with predictable naming
vulnerabilities.details: While using randomBytes(8) is good, temp files lack restrictive permissions. On multi-user systems, /tmp may be world-readable.

vulnerabilities.description: SQLite database file stored without encryption
vulnerabilities.details: Database at data/earnings_trades.db stores all trading history, P&L, and strategy information with no encryption.

vulnerabilities.description: Database credentials hardcoded in config file
vulnerabilities.details: dolthub_user: root stored in version control. If repo is shared, credentials could be exposed.

positiveFindings.description: Credentials properly stored using OS keyring backend
positiveFindings.impact: OAuth tokens and secrets protected by OS-level keyring, not plain text

positiveFindings.description: Parameterized queries used throughout (e.g., cursor.execute with %s placeholders)
execFileSync used instead of execSync to prevent shell injection
positiveFindings.impact: Git commands executed with array arguments, no shell interpolation

positiveFindings.description: GitHub body content routed through temporary files using --body-file
positiveFindings.impact: Prevents shell metacharacter injection in PR/issue bodies

riskScoreBreakdown.explanation: Low-severity plaintext storage findings (4 × 5pts = 20) + medium-severity config credentials (8pts) = 28
riskScoreBreakdown.context: Score is LOW overall because: (1) paper trading is default mode, (2) OAuth credentials properly protected in keyring, (3) SQL injection well-defended, (4) no hardcoded secrets in code.

recommendations.title: Move database credentials to environment variables
recommendations.action: Remove dolthub_user, dolthub_host, etc. from config.json. Load from os.environ instead. Keep config.example.json for reference only.
recommendations.impact: Prevents accidental credential exposure in version control

recommendations.title: Encrypt trading database at rest
recommendations.action: Use sqlcipher (SQLite encryption extension) or encrypt sensitive fields (entry_credit, exit_debit, pnl, legs_json) before storage.
recommendations.impact: Protects trading history and strategy results from disk access

recommendations.title: Encrypt session context before disk write
recommendations.action: In session.js, encrypt context data before atomicWrite(). Store only non-sensitive metadata on disk.
recommendations.impact: Prevents unauthorized access to session state

recommendations.title: Set restrictive permissions on temporary files
recommendations.action: In github-safe.js line 72, add fs.writeFileSync(tmpFile, body, {mode: 0o600})
recommendations.impact: Prevents other users from reading sensitive GitHub content in /tmp

recommendations.title: Validate broker connection before live trades
recommendations.action: Implement account number verification and credential check before Step 4b execution.
recommendations.impact: Prevents accidental trade execution on wrong account

recommendations.title: Audit logging for sensitive data exposure
recommendations.action: Review all print/console.log statements. Mask account numbers (last 4 digits only), avoid logging paths.

recommendations.title: Run dependency vulnerability scanning
recommendations.action: Execute 'npm audit' and 'pip audit' regularly. Set up automated checks.

summary.verdict: Well-architected with strong practices in critical areas. Main concerns are data-at-rest encryption and credential management.
weaknesses: No encryption at rest for trading data
summary.owasp: OWASP Top 10 (2021): A02:2021 Cryptographic Failures is primary concern; A03:2021 Injection well-defended; A05:2021 Access Control adequate for local tool
